Stream Card Style

Controls the typography and appearance of game cards in the sidebar preview and stream output. Adjust font sizes, team name color, logo size, and data refresh rate. For global defaults shared across all scoreboards, see Default Stream Settings.

Save Mode
Auto-save settings
When off, a Save button appears so you can review changes before applying.

Manual only

Colors for the status bar at the top of each game card.

Stream Section Headers
Headers that appear above each group of cards on the stream output (e.g. "🏎️ FORMULA 1 — UPCOMING", "⛳ PGA TOUR — THE MASTERS"). Includes the colored stripe on the left.

Data Source
ScoreStream uses the unofficial ESPN Scoreboard API. Data is fetched directly from your browser — no proxy needed.

If scores show "demo" it means ESPN blocked the request from your network (CORS). This is normal inside some Docker/reverse proxy setups.

Default Stream Settings

Global defaults that every scoreboard inherits for its stream output. Controls Theme, Font Sizes, Colors, and Card Size/Layout. Any scoreboard can override these individually in the scoreboard editor's Step 3 (Display & Layout).

Integrations

Connect ScoreStream to Dispatcharr to push live scoreboards as TV channels.

Dispatcharr Credentials
Stored securely in the ScoreStream API container — never in your browser.

Ticker Overlay

Overlay a live score ticker onto a Dispatcharr channel. Pick your sports, choose a channel, and enable — ScoreStream handles the rest.

⚠ Performance & Compatibility
The ticker overlay requires FFmpeg stream profiles — channels using other profile types are not supported. Enabling the ticker forces real-time video transcoding. This works with CPU-only encoding (libx264 ultrafast), but for optimal performance GPU hardware encoding (NVENC, VAAPI, QSV) is recommended. Without a GPU you may experience intermittent stuttering or frame drops.
Not active
📡 Dispatcharr Channel
Loading channels...
📺 Ticker Sources
Select sports to include. Enable FAVS on a sport to only show games involving your favorited teams for that sport.
Import from scoreboard:
PRO LEAGUES
MOTORSPORT
TENNIS
INTERNATIONAL SOCCER
NCAA MEN
NCAA WOMEN
🎨 Appearance
Position
Font size 28px
Scroll speed 150 px/s
0 = static (no scroll). Recommended: 100–200 px/s.
Background opacity 75%
👁 Live Preview
Updates as you adjust settings above.
VIDEO STREAM
⚡ Actions
Enabling creates a "Profile (Ticker)" copy in Dispatcharr and assigns it to the selected channel. Disabling restores the original profile and removes the copy.
⚠ Shared Volume Required
Score data is written to /ticker/scores.txt by the ScoreStream stream container. Both ScoreStream and Dispatcharr must mount the scorestream_ticker Docker volume at /ticker.

Backup & Restore

Export your ScoreStream configuration or restore from a previous backup.

Export Backup
Downloads a JSON file containing all your scoreboards, display settings, and Dispatcharr configuration. Does not include stream credentials.
Restore Backup
Upload a previously exported backup file. This will overwrite your current scoreboards and settings.
No file chosen

System Theme

Customize the ScoreStream app UI itself — the settings pages, sidebar, navigation, and controls. These colors and fonts affect only the interface you see here, not the stream output or game cards.

UI Colors
Each color is labeled with what it actually controls in the interface. Click any swatch to change it — changes apply instantly so you can see the effect.
UI Font Sizes
Scale text in the app interface. Sliders apply when you release the mouse (avoids the page jumping while you drag) and save automatically to your browser. The Save System Theme button only pushes the configuration to the server database (for survival across browser cache clears).
Base Font Size (everything) 15px
Sidebar Font Scale 1.00×
Topbar Meta Size 11px
Presets
Quick-apply a preset and customize from there.

Sports Library

Enable or disable sports globally. Enabled sports appear in the sidebar and can be toggled per scoreboard.

Enable or disable sports globally. Individual sports (PGA, F1, NASCAR, ATP, WTA) can be expanded ▸ to adjust their per-sport display settings (e.g. headshot size) for the main scoreboard view.
